dependencies: Aggregates have a high weight-to-price ratio that makes transportation expensive relative to the cost of material
Approximately 80% of total aggregates shipments are delivered exclusively from the producing location to the customer by truck
Production requires explosives, hydrocarbon fuels and electric power
Asphalt mix production requires liquid asphalt cement purchased from third-party producers
Ready-mixed concrete production requires cement purchased from third-party producers
risks: Availability and cost of trucks, railcars, barges and ships, as well as their licensed operators, for transport of materials
Truck driver and rail crew shortages
Capacity constraints and minimum tonnage requirements
Rail service interruptions or rate increases

regulatory_risks: Zoning and permitting regulations curtail expansion in certain areas
Stricter laws and regulations, or more stringent interpretations of existing laws or regulations, may impose new liabilities, taxes or tariffs

Regulations may reduce operating hours, require additional investment in pollution control equipment, create restrictions on products, impede access to reserves or hamper opening of new or expansion of existing plants or facilities
Federal, state and local laws and regulations relating to protection of environment and worker health and safety
Regulation of facility air emissions and water discharges
Waste management regulations
Protection of wetlands, listed and threatened species
Noise and dust exposure control for workers
Safety regulations under Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA) and Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)
Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act (CERCLA or Superfund)
Climate change legislation or regulations may adversely impact business including provisions for cap and trade system or carbon tax
Changes in tax laws, guidance and interpretations
Elimination of deductions (particularly the depletion deduction) or credits
Expectations relating to environmental, social and governance considerations and related reporting obligations
Federal, state or international laws and regulations related to sustainability goals
Claims that products do not meet regulatory requirements or contractual specifications
Building code or other regulatory requirements for products
Changes in international trade policies such as United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA)
Imposition of duties, tariffs, taxes or government royalties
Arbitrary changes to permits, zoning classifications or operating agreements

sanction_risks: Mexican government has taken actions adverse to property and operations in Mexico including arbitrary shutdown orders to immediately cease underwater quarrying and extraction operations at Calica operations in Quintana Roo, Mexico (May 2022)
Mexican government suspended three-year customs permit granted to Calica and began proceeding that could result in revocation of that permit (May 2022)
Mexican government ordered closure of Calica's already-suspended quarrying activities and shutdown of certain activities at Calica's Punta Venado port facilities (September 2024)

President of Mexico signed presidential decree declaring entirety of Calica's properties as Natural Protected Area (ANP Decree) prohibiting extraction of petrous or construction materials (September 2024)

NAFTA arbitration claim filed against United Mexican States for treatment of quarrying operations in Quintana Roo, Mexico arising from Mexico's failure to comply with legally binding zoning agreement and other unfair, arbitrary and capricious actions
